site.title: Yusuf İhsan Görgel — Software Engineer
site.description: Yusuf İhsan Görgel is a software engineer building cross-platform products with Flutter, Dart, Go, PostgreSQL, Redis, and production infrastructure.
site.social_description: Cross-platform product engineering, selected professional work, and verified open-source contributions by Yusuf İhsan Görgel.

profile.name: Yusuf İhsan Görgel
profile.headline: I build cross-platform software, then stay for the hard parts.
profile.summary: I work across Flutter, Dart, and Go, turning product ideas into software that holds up on real devices, unreliable networks, and long-lived release cycles.
profile.background: Since 2022 I have shipped mobile, desktop, and web products for retail, logistics, and digital publishing. My work has moved from client applications and native integrations into offline data, real-time synchronisation, backend services, release engineering, and the production systems around them.

experience.summary: Build and maintain production applications across Android and iOS tablets plus Linux, macOS, and Windows desktops. I work on offline-first SQLite and Drift data, native integrations, localisation, real-time synchronisation, release engineering, and reliability on point-of-sale hardware.
Delivered mobile and web product work spanning Flutter and React, REST and WebSocket data flows, live vehicle tracking, Google and Apple Maps integrations, deployment, and QA coordination.
Shipped the Aydınlık E-Gazete and Bilim ve Ütopya E-Dergi applications for Android and iOS with maintainable presentation boundaries, API integration, localisation, and store-ready release workflows.
Formal study in algorithms, data structures, databases, software design, and team-based product development.

contributions.title: Make Firebase core loading deterministic on WebKit
contributions.problem: Component bundles could execute before firebase-app.js was available, producing an initialization race in WebKit browsers.
contributions.change: Load the Firebase core script before component bundles and preserve deterministic dependency order.

contributions.title: Reset unknown dropdown initial values on first build
contributions.problem: A dropdown could begin with a value that did not exist in its items and fail before normal field reset behaviour ran.
contributions.change: Normalize the first build and cover the regression with widget tests.

contributions.title: Treat SQLite TRUE and 1 defaults as the same schema
contributions.problem: Schema verification reported equivalent SQLite boolean defaults as a migration difference.
contributions.change: Normalize the verifier comparison and add regression coverage for both representations.

contributions.title: Add a Fiber and Asynq background-jobs recipe
contributions.problem: The recipe collection lacked a production-shaped example for enqueueing and processing Redis-backed work outside request handlers.
contributions.change: Add a runnable producer, worker, queue configuration, and usage documentation.

contributions.title: Wait for web rendering before the first-frame event
contributions.problem: On SkWasm, flutter-first-frame can fire before the compositor presents visible Flutter content, leaving a measurable blank handoff window.
contributions.change: Wait for outstanding scene renders and the next browser frame before dispatching the event; the pull request is ready for review with seven Chrome renderer and compiler suites passing.

contributions.title: Add SEP-2575 request metadata and discovery types
contributions.problem: The SDK needed a wire model for request-scoped protocol and client context without breaking legacy request shapes.
contributions.change: Add typed experimental metadata accessors, server discovery types, polymorphic codecs, and malformed-input coverage.

systems.name: Dorse
systems.summary: A logistics platform connecting drivers, fleet operators, and weighbridge businesses through mobile and web workflows.
systems.ownership: I worked across the Flutter application and React web surface, including live vehicle state, maps, API flows, deployment, and QA coordination.
systems.decision: Keep real-time vehicle and weighbridge state understandable across clients instead of hiding it behind screen-specific workarounds.
systems.challenge: Drivers, fleet operators, and weighbridge businesses needed to act on the same changing logistics state across mobile and web workflows.
systems.approach: Work across Flutter and React clients, maps, REST and WebSocket flows, deployment, and QA so vehicle and weighbridge state stayed explicit from transport to interface.
systems.outcome: Delivered a public logistics product spanning driver, fleet, and weighbridge workflows on mobile and web.

systems.name: Queue Inspector MCP
systems.summary: An MCP server for inspecting and operating Redis-backed Asynq and BullMQ queues without reaching into storage by hand.
systems.ownership: I designed the command surface, typed validation, queue adapters, and conservative state-changing operations.
systems.decision: Observation stays read-only by default; retries and destructive operations remain explicit and narrowly scoped.
systems.challenge: Queue incidents are described in jobs, states, attempts, and errors, while generic Redis inspection exposes low-level keys and a broader mutation surface.
systems.approach: Build typed MCP tools and backend-specific adapters for Asynq, BullMQ, and Sidekiq, with a read-only mode that removes mutating tools from discovery.
systems.outcome: Published a six-tool server with a recorded, non-mocked queue recovery flow and explicit safeguards around retries and deletion.

systems.name: Multi-tenant Gateway
systems.summary: A small single-binary gateway that demonstrates tenant resolution, JWT authentication, per-tenant rate limits, and module boundaries.
systems.ownership: I designed and implemented the complete reference from transport and tenant resolution through policy and test coverage.
systems.decision: Keep tenant context explicit from transport through policy rather than hiding it behind global state.
systems.challenge: A small team needs tenant isolation and shared policy without multiplying services, deploys, and failure surfaces for every product module.
systems.approach: Carry resolved tenant context through one middleware chain, isolate persistent data by schema, and make PostgreSQL and Redis production wiring opt-in without changing module boundaries.
systems.outcome: Published a single-binary reference with tests for tenant resolution, authentication, rate limiting, schema isolation, and migration behaviour.

systems.name: Aydınlık E-Gazete
systems.summary: An iPhone and iPad newspaper application with issue reading, API-backed content, localisation, and release workflows.
systems.ownership: I delivered Flutter client work across presentation, API integration, and store-ready releases.
systems.decision: Keep the reading experience responsive while the content model and release process remain easy to maintain.

systems.name: Bilim ve Ütopya E-Dergi
systems.summary: A cross-platform magazine reader for browsing and reading digital issues on iPhone and iPad.
systems.ownership: I built Flutter client features, API integration, localisation, and mobile release workflows.
systems.decision: Use a shared presentation model across platforms while preserving store-specific release behaviour.

systems.name: Redis Task Queue
systems.summary: A Redis-backed task queue for Dart with retries, dead-letter handling, and weighted queues.
systems.ownership: I designed the public API, queue semantics, failure handling, and runnable examples.
systems.decision: Make failure states inspectable and scheduling behaviour deterministic without adding a runtime framework.

systems.name: Constellation Particles
systems.summary: A dependency-free particle field for Flutter with pointer interaction and spatial-grid neighbour lookup.
systems.ownership: I implemented the painter, pointer interaction, spatial partitioning, and package examples without runtime dependencies.
systems.decision: Use spatial partitioning to avoid comparing every particle pair as the scene grows.

systems.name: Flutter Web Portfolio
systems.summary: A production Flutter Web portfolio used to investigate first-frame timing, semantic structure, locale transitions, and release integrity.
systems.ownership: I built the content schema, Flutter interface, renderer handoff, browser tests, runtime budgets, and deployment path.
systems.decision: Keep the semantic document separate from high-frequency rendering while both follow the same measured scroll geometry.
